response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack engineer with over two decades of software experience, including genuinely strong software-level performance work (low-latency DLang services handling millions of requests per second) and some C/C++ and embedded exposure. However, the role is a hardware-centric Principal Performance Architect position requiring SoC and IP modeling, GPU/accelerator architecture knowledge, SIMD/MIMD experience, and deep-learning training/inference optimization — none of which appear anywhere in his background. His career trajectory is web, mobile, and platform engineering, not silicon or systems architecture, so the domain gap is too large for a principal-level hardware role despite his seniority.
strengths: Strong software performance engineering background (low-latency, async I/O, high-throughput systems in DLang and C++)
Deep and versatile engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems with a testing-first craftsmanship ethos
Recent exposure to AI tooling (MCP code generators and agentic-workflow infrastructure)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or system-on-chip architecture experience
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep neural network training/inference optimization background
Career focus is full-stack/product software, far from the hardware performance-architecture domain of this principal-level role

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware co-design experience
No GPU/accelerator or DNN workload optimization background
